scenario.summary: Monday batch — 847 invoices, duplicates, FX, missing POs
scenario.situation: Monday morning. A batch of 847 supplier invoices from the past week needs processing. The AP team of 6 handles ~3,200 invoices per month across all entities. The manager needs to clear this batch by Wednesday — two team members are out this week. The batch includes invoices from GlobalSteel (high volume, usually clean), Shenzhen Precision (frequent price variances due to FX), and a handful of new vendors that Houston onboarded last month.
dataInputs: Incoming invoice batch (PDFs, EDI, email attachments)
Historical invoice data for pattern matching
agentSteps: Extracts invoice data from mixed formats (PDF, EDI, email) using OCR and document understanding
Performs 3-way matching (invoice, PO, goods receipt) — auto-matches 612 of 847 invoices (72%)
Flags INV-88202 (Shenzhen): price variance of +3.2% vs. PO — determines 2.1% is FX-driven and 1.1% is a genuine price increase, routes to manager with context
Flags INV-88203 (Summit Logistics): no PO on file, searches for matching contracts or blanket POs, finds none — routes to Houston procurement for PO creation
Catches INV-88206 (GlobalSteel): duplicate of INV-88201 — same PO, same amount, same vendor, 2 days apart. Blocks payment and alerts the manager
Auto-approves INV-88205 (DataForge): below $15K threshold, clean match, recurring vendor
Handles INV-88204 (Rheinmetall): converts EUR 67,300 at current ECB rate, applies to correct GL entity (2000)
scenario.expectedOutput: 612 invoices auto-processed, 4 flagged for human review with context and recommended actions, 1 duplicate blocked, processing time reduced from 3 days to same-day for clean invoices
